attributes.action: Remove the protected status of gulls breeds.
attributes.additional_details:
Lots of people in my area and the whole of Great Britain have been attacked by protected breeds of gulls and some have been hospitalised and now have trauma from them. They are a danger to society and wildlife and something needs to be done before it get worse.
Councils and local authorities are forced to spend money from limited funds to deter gulls from breeding and settling, but this costs a lot for a not guaranteed result and can take multiple seasons to be effective.
attributes.background:
Remove the protected status on gull breeds like seagulls to allow controlled culling to take place in order to reduce the population and breeding, which has grown beyond sensible and safe numbers.
This could be done alongside environmental health people to endure safety for locals and the area.

rejection.details:
Your petition is about something that the Scottish Government and Scottish Parliament is responsible for. That means that the UK Government and Parliament can't look into it. 

The Scottish Parliament has its own Public Petitions Committee. If you'd like to send your petition to the Scottish Parliament, you can find out more here: https://petitions.parliament.scot/

You may also wish to contact your local council and MSP who represent you about this issue. 

You can find the name of your local council and how to contact them here: https://www.mygov.scot/find-your-local-council

You can find the name of your local MSP and how to contact them here: https://www.parliament.scot/msps
